perturbation.original_text: You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed acquisition. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'transaction' to 'acquisition' at the begining of this section, while later in the same section retaining the phrase 'proposed transaction' creates an in-text contradiction, because it becomes unclear if the 'proposed transaction' refers to the aquisition or something else. This affects what the Confidential Evaluation Material can be used for.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed acquisition. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'transaction' to 'acquisition' at the beginning of the section. However, the phrase 'proposed transaction' is retained later in the same section (and the contradicted text). This creates an in-text contradiction because it becomes unclear whether 'proposed transaction' refers to the acquisition or something else, affecting the permitted use of the Confidential Evaluation Material. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: In the event you or your representatives receive a request to disclose Confidential Evaluation Material pursuant to any court order or legal process, you or your representatives will give the Company prompt written notice thereof so that the Company may seek an appropriate protective order. You and your representatives agree to cooperate (at the Company’s expense) as reasonably requested by the Company in its efforts to maintain the confidentiality of such Confidential Evaluation Material. If you or your representatives are ultimately required to disclose such Confidential Evaluation Material, you or your representatives shall disclose only so much thereof as necessary to comply with such court order or legal process.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event you or your representatives receive a request to disclose Confidential Evaluation Data pursuant to any court order or legal process, you or your representatives will give the Company prompt written notice thereof so that the Company may seek an appropriate protective order. You and your representatives agree to cooperate (at the Company’s expense) as reasonably requested by the Company in its efforts to maintain the confidentiality of such Confidential Evaluation Material. If you or your representatives are ultimately required to disclose such Confidential Evaluation Material, you or your representatives shall disclose only so much thereof as necessary to comply with such court order or legal process.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Evaluation Material' to 'Confidential Evaluation Data' at the beginning of this section and keeping 'Confidential Evaluation Material' in subsequent references creates confusion about what needs to be disclosed under legal processes. The term has been defined as 'Confidential Evaluation Material', contradicting this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event you or your representatives receive a request to disclose Confidential Evaluation Data pursuant to any court order or legal process, you or your representatives will give the Company prompt written notice thereof so that the Company may seek an appropriate protective order. You and your representatives agree to cooperate (at the Company’s expense) as reasonably requested by the Company in its efforts to maintain the confidentiality of such Confidential Evaluation Material. If you or your representatives are ultimately required to disclose such Confidential Evaluation Material, you or your representatives shall disclose only so much thereof as necessary to comply with such court order or legal process.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Evaluation Material' with 'Confidential Evaluation Data' at the beginning of the section, while subsequent references still use 'Confidential Evaluation Material'. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the obligation to disclose applies to both 'Confidential Evaluation Data' and 'Confidential Evaluation Material', or only to the latter, as explained by the Perturbation Explanation. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location. The original text used 'Confidential Evaluation Material' consistently, while the changed text creates inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: Except as may be required by court order or legal process, neither party hereto nor any of its representatives will disclose to any other person or entity: (a) the fact that information regarding the Company is being or has been furnished to you; (b) the fact that discussions or negotiations regarding any transaction are or have been taking place between representatives of you and the Company, or any information regarding the status or terms of any such discussions or negotiations between the Company and you or the identity of the parties thereto; (c) the fact that the Company is or has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction with you of the nature discussed by you and the Company. In addition, neither you nor your representatives will disclose to any other person or entity that the Company has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company, with any third party. Either party hereto shall be permitted to disclose any fact or information covered by this paragraph 4 to the same extent, and under the same conditions, as you are permitted to disclose Confidential Evaluation Material pursuant to paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof. Nothing in this paragraph (4) shall be construed to prevent or limit the Company’s or its representatives’ right to disclose, discuss, negotiate or enter into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company with any other person or entity or provide information regarding the Company to any other person or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as may be required by court order or legal process, neither party hereto nor any of its representatives will disclose to any other person or entity: (a) the fact that information regarding the Business is being or has been furnished to you; (b) the fact that discussions or negotiations regarding any transaction are or have been taking place between representatives of you and the Company, or any information regarding the status or terms of any such discussions or negotiations between the Company and you or the identity of the parties thereto; (c) the fact that the Company is or has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction with you of the nature discussed by you and the Company. In addition, neither you nor your representatives will disclose to any other person or entity that the Company has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company, with any third party. Either party hereto shall be permitted to disclose any fact or information covered by this paragraph 4 to the same extent, and under the same conditions, as you are permitted to disclose Confidential Evaluation Material pursuant to paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof. Nothing in this paragraph (4) shall be construed to prevent or limit the Company’s or its representatives’ right to disclose, discuss, negotiate or enter into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company with any other person or entity or provide information regarding the Company to any other person or entity.
perturbation.explanation: In section (4), changing 'Company' to 'Business' in subsection (a), but not in subsequent subsections (b) and (c), or in the rest of the paragraph creates an in-text contradiction. It is now unclear whether the restrictions on disclosing information apply equally to both 'Company' and 'Business,' leading to potential confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as may be required by court order or legal process, neither party hereto nor any of its representatives will disclose to any other person or entity: (a) the fact that information regarding the Business is being or has been furnished to you; (b) the fact that discussions or negotiations regarding any transaction are or have been taking place between representatives of you and the Company, or any information regarding the status or terms of any such discussions or negotiations between the Company and you or the identity of the parties thereto; (c) the fact that the Company is or has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction with you of the nature discussed by you and the Company. In addition, neither you nor your representatives will disclose to any other person or entity that the Company has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company, with any third party. Either party hereto shall be permitted to disclose any fact or information covered by this paragraph 4 to the same extent, and under the same conditions, as you are permitted to disclose Confidential Evaluation Material pursuant to paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof. Nothing in this paragraph (4) shall be construed to prevent or limit the Company’s or its representatives’ right to disclose, discuss, negotiate or enter into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company with any other person or entity or provide information regarding the Company to any other person or entity.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a contradiction. The original text consistently refers to 'Company' when discussing disclosure restrictions. The changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Business' in subsection (a) but not in subsections (b) and (c). This creates uncertainty as to whether the disclosure restrictions apply to both the 'Company' and the 'Business,' leading to an in-text contradiction as defined by Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon the Company’s request, all Confidential Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) shall be returned or, if you so choose, destroyed and not retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason and written certification to that effect will be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Confidential Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives for the sole purpose of compliance with such law, regulation, policy or procedures shall not be used for any purpose other than to evidence compliance therewith and such retained material and information shall continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this letter agreement, which obligations shall survive any termination hereof. All the Confidential Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon the Firm’s request, all Confidential Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) shall be returned or, if you so choose, destroyed and not retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason and written certification to that effect will be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Confidential Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives for the sole purpose of compliance with such law, regulation, policy or procedures shall not be used for any purpose other than to evidence compliance therewith and such retained material and information shall continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this letter agreement, which obligations shall survive any termination hereof. All the Confidential Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Company' to 'Firm' at the start of this section, while still referencing back to the same entity as 'the Company' later in the paragraph creates confusion. It is unclear whether a request from 'the Firm' is equivalent to a request from 'the Company', especially since the original agreement defines the 'Company'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At any time upon the Firm’s request, all Confidential Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) shall be returned or, if you so choose, destroyed and not retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason and written certification to that effect will be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Confidential Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives for the sole purpose of compliance with such law, regulation, policy or procedures shall not be used for any purpose other than to evidence compliance therewith and such retained material and information shall continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this letter agreement, which obligations shall survive any termination hereof. All the Confidential Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Firm' at the beginning of the section while still referring to 'the Company' later in the same paragraph. This creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity about which entity is making the request. The contradicted text is the same section where the change occurs, which underscores the in-text nature of the contradiction. This misalignment of terminology directly creates confusion, matching the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, neither you nor your representatives will, without the prior written consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business that does not in any event result in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, neither you nor your representatives will, without the prior written consent of the Organization, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business that does not in any event result in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Company' to 'Organization' at the beginning of the section (10), while leaving the rest of the section referencing 'Company', creates an internal contradiction. Since the term 'Company' is defined in this agreement, it is now ambiguous whether the consent requirement applies to the 'Organization', the 'Company', or both, affecting the restrictions on acquiring securities and influencing management.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, neither you nor your representatives will, without the prior written consent of the Organization, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business that does not in any event result in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Company' to 'Organization' in the Changed Text, while the rest of the section still refers to 'Company', creates a contradiction. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, but it highlights the core issue: the requirement for consent from the 'Organization' now exists, but the restrictions relate to the 'Company'. The original text had no such contradiction. This misalignment of terminology, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, introduces ambiguity and directly creates an in-text contradiction.
